name: Ritual Bronze Ding
description: Ceremonial tripod vessel with taotie mask motif; used for meat offerings at ancestral rites.

name: Celadon Longquan Vase
description: Longquan kiln vase with pooled jade-green glaze and subtle carved lotus scrolling.

name: Gilt-Bronze Buddha
museum: Metropolitan Museum of Art, New York
description: Small votive figure with flame-shaped aureole and traces of mercury gilding on the robes.

name: Famille Rose Plate
description: Export-style plate with famille rose enamels depicting peony sprays and gilt rim.

name: Oracle Bone Fragment
description: Inscribed oracle bone registering a divination on rainfall and barley harvest.

name: Landscape Handscroll
description: Mountain-and-water motif with dispersed temples; album leaves originally from a court set.

name: White Jade Bi Disc
museum: Freer Gallery of Art, Washington D.C.
description: Perforated ritual disc carved with swirling grain-pattern relief on both faces.

name: Dragon Robe Panel
description: Robe fragment with five-clawed dragon chasing a flaming pearl among clouds.

name: Zhou Ritual Gui Vessel
museum: Freer Gallery of Art, Washington D.C.
description: Late Western Zhou inscribed gui paired typologically with tripod ding forms.
